research_notes.primary_sources: Polymarket market rules page for 'Colombia Presidential Election'; Registraduria 2026 presidential calendar; Reuters on 6 and 8 March 2026 elections/primaries; AS/COA Poll Tracker updated 16 March 2026.
research_notes.historical_precedents: Colombia uses a two-round presidential system; historically, nationally visible figures with strong ideological lanes and coalition capacity dominate, while low-visibility aspirants almost never convert from far behind into a presidential win.
research_notes.institutional_constraints: First round is scheduled for 31 May 2026 and runoff for 21 June 2026 if no candidate clears 50%. The market resolves to the listed candidate who wins, including any second round, with fallback to Registraduria if needed.
research_notes.timeline_recent: 6 March 2026: Reuters previewed a fragmented field and highlighted Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella as notable non-primary contenders. 8 March 2026: Reuters reported Paloma Valencia won the right-wing primary, Roy Barreras the left primary, and Claudia López the centrist primary. 16 March 2026: AS/COA described Cepeda as leading most polls, de la Espriella just behind, and Valencia benefiting from primary momentum.
research_notes.actors_and_incentives: Juan Manuel Galán: Centrist establishment surname with modest but non-leading profile.
research_notes.key_uncertainties: Runoff matchups, coalition endorsements between rounds, turnout composition, late security shocks, and whether anti-Petro or pro-continuity sentiment hardens before 31 May.
research_notes.counterarguments: Juan Manuel Galán is not impossible; Colombia's field is fragmented and late coalition shocks can change runoff entrants.
research_notes.dominant_market_narrative: The contract is priced as a low-probability outcome because Juan Manuel Galán is not currently described in March reporting as a leading contender.
